Currently, the app has a standard launcher, but needs the `LEANBACK_LAUNCHER` for Android TV.  UI navigation lacks focus management for D-pad control, essential for TV remotes. I'm now implementing the complete Leanback feature. I've updated `AndroidManifest.xml` with the necessary intent filters and feature flags for Android TV compatibility. To provide full **Android TV** capability, we currently have the foundational "Android Kernel" (`AndroidManifest.xml`), but it requires three specific technical upgrades to be recognized as a "Leanback" (TV-compatible) application and to be navigable via a standard TV remote (D-pad).

### Specification: Android TV Integration

1.  **Manifest Calibration**:
    *   Update `AndroidManifest.xml` to include the `LEANBACK_LAUNCHER` category. This ensures the app appears in the Android TV app row.
    *   Declare `android.software.leanback` as a requirement (set to `false` to maintain mobile compatibility).
    *   Add `android.hardware.touchscreen` as not required, as TVs utilize D-pad input.

2.  **Kinetic Focus Engine (UI/UX)**:
    *   Currently, the interface relies on touch/click events. For TV, we need visible **Focus States**.
    *   Update `theme.css` with global `:focus-visible` selectors. When an operator moves the D-pad, the active button or card will pulse with the signature `--app-accent` glow, providing the "10-foot UI" feedback necessary for distance viewing.

3.  **Bento Grid Scaling**:
    *   The `GameHubPage` and `MainContent` already use grid layouts that translate well to widescreen, but the focus styles will ensure that "trapped focus" (where you can't get back to the header) is avoided.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.thevoid">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    
    <!-- Required for Android TV to identify the app as a Leanback experience -->
    <uses-feature android:name="android.software.leanback" android:required="false" />
    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.touchscreen" android:required="false" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:banner="@drawable/banner"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.TheVoid">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:configChanges="orientation|screenSize|keyboardHidden">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
                <!-- This enables the app to show up on the Android TV home screen -->
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LEANBACK_LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
    </application>

</manifest>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.thevoid">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    
    <!-- Identify as a Leanback (TV) experience -->
    <uses-feature android:name="android.software.leanback" android:required="false" />
    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.touchscreen" android:required="false" />
    <!-- Required for D-Pad controllers -->
    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.type.television" android:required="false" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:banner="@drawable/banner"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.TheVoid">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:screenOrientation="landscape"
            android:configChanges="orientation|screenSize|keyboardHidden">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
                <!-- Essential for showing up in the TV App Row -->
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LEANBACK_LAUNCHER" />

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.thevoid">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    
    <!-- Identify as a Leanback (TV) experience -->
    <uses-feature android:name="android.software.leanback" android:required="false" />
    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.touchscreen" android:required="false" />
    <!-- Required for D-Pad controllers -->
    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.type.television" android:required="false" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:banner="@drawable/banner"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.TheVoid">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:screenOrientation="fullSensor"
            android:configChanges="orientation|screenSize|keyboardHidden">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
                <!-- Essential for showing up in the TV App Row -->
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LEANBACK_LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
    </application>

</manifest>
